body:
## Goal
Provide a dependency-free Node.js HTTP server that answers GET / with an HTML Hello, World! response, launchable via npm start.

## Spec
Server built on Node's built-in `node:http` module only — no external web framework, no runtime dependencies. ESM (`"type": "module"` in package.json; the source file uses import/export syntax). Listens on `process.env.PORT` parsed as a number, defaulting to 3000 when unset. GET / responds 200 with header `Content-Type: text/html` and a body containing the exact substring `Hello, World!`. package.json declares a `start` script that launches the single source file with `node`. Implementation confined to one source file plus package.json.

## Changes
- `server.js` — creates
- `package.json` — refactors-existing

## Acceptance
- [ ] AC-1: package.json sets "type": "module" and declares a "start" script that launches the server source file with node
- [ ] AC-2: The single source file imports only Node's built-in node:http module and package.json lists no runtime dependencies
- [ ] AC-3: The server listens on the port from process.env.PORT, defaulting to 3000 when the variable is unset
- [ ] AC-4: A GET request to / returns HTTP status 200 with a Content-Type header of text/html and a response body containing the exact text "Hello, World!"

## Verify
- PORT=3000 node server.js & sleep 1; curl -s -i http://localhost:3000/ | grep -q '200' && curl -s -i http://localhost:3000/ | grep -qi 'content-type: text/html' && curl -s http://localhost:3000/ | grep -q 'Hello, World!'; kill %1 (e2e)

## Non-Goals
- No external web framework (Express, Fastify, etc.) or other runtime dependencies
- No routing beyond GET /
- No TLS/HTTPS, logging, or configuration beyond the PORT env var

<!-- meta: {"reason_to_exist":"Establish a minimal, framework-free HTTP entry point that serves the Hello, World! response."} -->

title: Minimal ESM HTTP server returning Hello, World! on GET /
